clinical_notes.summary: 63yo female with HTN and hypothyroidism presenting with 2-week history of intermittent irregular heartbeat, fatigue, and new exertional dyspnea. Episodes of chest 'fluttering' lasting minutes to hours, first noticed while checking pulse at home. Denies syncope, chest pain, or symptoms of thyroid dysfunction. TSH normal 3 months ago. Medications: Amlodipine 10mg daily, Levothyroxine 75mcg daily. Allergy: iodine-based contrast. Family history: mother with AFib at age 70, father with stroke at 75. HR 92 bpm (irregular), BP 138/84 mmHg. Exam: irregularly irregular pulse, variable S1 intensity, no murmurs, lungs clear.

chest_xray.summary: Mild cardiomegaly with cardiothoracic ratio 0.54. Mild left atrial enlargement suggested by straightening of the left heart border. No pulmonary vascular congestion. Lung fields clear. No pleural effusions. Aortic knob mildly calcified consistent with age and hypertension. No acute abnormality.

ecg.summary: Atrial fibrillation with moderate ventricular response at 92 bpm. Irregularly irregular RR intervals. Absence of discrete P waves — fibrillatory baseline. Left axis deviation. QRS 88ms (normal). No ST-segment changes or T-wave inversions. QTc 410ms. No pre-excitation. LVH criteria not met by voltage. New AFib compared with prior if available.

echo.summary: Preserved LVEF 58%. Left atrium mildly dilated at 4.1cm (normal <4.0cm), consistent with new-onset atrial fibrillation substrate. Normal LV size and wall thickness. Diastolic dysfunction grade I (impaired relaxation). No significant valvular disease — trace mitral regurgitation. Normal right heart. No thrombus identified in left atrial appendage (limited assessment without TEE). No pericardial effusion.

heart_sounds.summary: Irregularly irregular rhythm at approximately 92 bpm consistent with atrial fibrillation. Variable intensity of S1 due to variable diastolic filling time. S2 normal. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs. No S3 or S4. Cardiac auscultation findings consistent with hemodynamically stable atrial fibrillation.

lab_results.summary: TSH 2.1 mIU/L (normal — thyroid dysfunction excluded as AFib trigger). Free T4 1.2 ng/dL (normal). CBC: WBC 7.2 x10³/µL, Hemoglobin 13.4 g/dL. BMP: sodium 140 mEq/L, potassium 4.1 mEq/L, creatinine 0.9 mg/dL, eGFR 72 mL/min. BNP 142 pg/mL (mildly elevated, consistent with new AFib). Magnesium 2.1 mEq/L. CRP 8 mg/L (mildly elevated). Fasting glucose 96 mg/dL.

medication.summary: Active medications: Amlodipine 10mg daily (hypertension), Levothyroxine 75mcg daily (hypothyroidism). Allergy: iodine contrast. Initiated for new-onset AFib: Apixaban 5mg BID (anticoagulation — CHA2DS2-VASc score 3: age, female, HTN), Metoprolol succinate 50mg daily (rate control). Note: rate-control target HR <110 bpm at rest. Cardioversion to be considered after adequate anticoagulation. Amlodipine continued for blood pressure management.
